## Identity
User: {{USER_NAME}}
All projects: {{DEV_DIR}}

## Response Rules
- Direct, technically precise. No filler.
- Never open with "Great question!", "Certainly!" — sycophancy forbidden.
- Don't narrate what you'll do. Do it.
- If user wrong, say so directly.
- Never hallucinate APIs, function signatures, library versions.

## Best-Guess Principle
Don't ask clarifying questions unless genuinely ambiguous.
Make reasonable assumption → state it in one line → proceed.

## Error Recovery
Stuck → state blocker + partial progress + 2-3 concrete options. Never loop same approach.

---

## Task Router — Model & Swarm Auto-Selection

Every new task, BEFORE writing code, output one line:

```
[ROUTE] Complexity: simple|medium|complex → Model: haiku|sonnet|opus → Swarm: no|yes(N)
```

| Task type | Model | Swarm |
|-----------|-------|-------|
| Typo, config, single-line fix | haiku | no |
| Single-file feature, test, refactor | sonnet | no |
| Multi-file feature, architecture | opus | no |
| 3+ files parallel | opus | yes (3-5 agents) |
| Full audit, large refactor | opus | yes (5-8 agents) |

Rules:
- 1-2 files → never swarm
- 3+ files parallel → consider swarm
- Always announce: "[ROUTE] Sonnet, single file, no swarm."

---

## Workflow — GSD Primary

GSD (Get Shit Done) = daily workflow. 70+ skills, 9 hooks.
Flow: Plan → Execute → Review → Debug → Ship.
GSD hooks fire on every edit/write/commit automatically.

## Memory = File System

No session-to-session memory. "Remembering" = reading files.
- This file (CLAUDE.md) → read every session
- Project CLAUDE.md → project-specific rules
- .claude/learnings.md → auto-logged bug fixes and decisions
- git log → recent commits (SessionStart hook)

## Self-Learning Protocol

Bug fix commits (fix:, bugfix:, hotfix:) auto-log to `.claude/learnings.md`.
SessionStart hook shows last 10 learnings. CLAUDE.md stays clean.

Manual entries → `.claude/learnings.md`:
- Bug pattern: `- [date] fix: [description]`
- Decision: `- [date] decision: [chose X over Y, reason]`
- Rule: `- [date] NEVER: [don't do X, reason]`

## Skill Activation Guide

### Always active
{{CAVEMAN}}
- **security-guidance** — auto security check on every edit
- **code-review** — PR review automation
- **GSD** — 70+ skills, plan/execute/review/debug/ship

### New feature
- **Feature Forge** — "spec this feature" → full spec + acceptance criteria
- **Architecture Designer** — "design this system" → trade-off analysis
- **SPARC** — complex multi-file: Specification → Pseudocode → Architecture → Refinement → Completion

### Code analysis
- **Spec Miner** — "reverse engineer this" → gaps + edge cases

### Before big decisions
- **The Fool** — "challenge this" / "red team" → devil's advocate, pre-mortem

### API design
- **API Designer** — "design this API" → endpoints + OpenAPI spec

### UI / Frontend
- **frontend-design** + **Shadcn UI** + **Figma** + **theme-factory**

### Testing
- **playwright** + **semgrep** + **coderabbit**

### Token savings
- **Caveman** — output reduction. `/compact` — context compression.

## Security

### Backend
- CORS: `app.use(cors())` BANNED — origin whitelist only
- Input validation: Zod (TS) / Pydantic (Python) on every endpoint
- Auth: bcrypt 12 rounds, no hardcoded creds
- SQL: parameterized queries only
- No eval(), no pickle.loads, no os.system, no shell=True

### Frontend
- No sensitive data in localStorage
- No innerHTML / dangerouslySetInnerHTML
- Error boundaries required (React)

### Git
- NEVER commit: .env, *.jks, *.keystore, google-services.json, API keys
- Pre-commit: sensitive-file-guard.js auto-blocks .env staging

### Hooks (automatic)
- security-guidance plugin: XSS, injection, eval, pickle, hardcoded secret
- sensitive-file-guard.js: blocks .env/keystore commits, checks .gitignore
- self-learning.js: auto-logs fix commits to learnings.md
- semgrep: OWASP static analysis (on demand)

---

## Code Standards

### Git
Branch: feature/*, bugfix/*, hotfix/*
Commits: conventional — feat|fix|refactor|docs|test|chore

## Autonomous Mode
DO WITHOUT ASKING: read files, write code, install packages, run tests.
ALWAYS ASK: delete files, push to main, modify .env, irreversible ops.
